name: County Cork Irish Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Season lamb chops with salt and pepper.
2. Heat oil in a wide saucepan large enough to hold all chops in a single layer.
3. Brown the chops on both sides.
4. Spoon off any melted fat.
5. Add enough water to cover the chops. Bring to a boil.
6. Add parsley, bay leaf, peppercorns, thyme, and rosemary (enclosed in cheesecloth).
7. Lower heat and simmer.
8. Peel potatoes and shape into bite-sized rounds.
9. Chop trimmings from potatoes into small pieces.
10. Add potatoes, trimmings, cabbage, onion, well-rinsed leek, white onions, and celery to chops and liquid.
11. Simmer for 20 minutes.
12. Add peas. If needed, add a little more water during cooking.
13. Continue simmering for another 10 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
14. Correct seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary.
15. Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.
